title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Authentic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for a satisfying lunch or dinner. Get cooking!
longTailKeywords: best Butter Chicken recipe at home
seo.h1: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of creamy, rich Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro, served with naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the velvety richness of homemade Butter Chicken, a classic Indian Murgh Makhani dish that's surprisingly easy to make!

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a world-renowned Indian dish celebrated for its incredibly rich, creamy, and mildly spiced tomato-based gravy. Tender pieces of marinated chicken are simmered in this luscious sauce, offering a delightful balance of tanginess and sweetness. It is a quintessential comfort food that promises a truly satisfying culinary experience.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 min

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, 1 tsp garam masala, lemon juice, and salt. Mix well to coat the chicken thoroughly. Cover and marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Sear the chicken for 3-4 minutes on each side until lightly browned and partially cooked. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt 2 tbsp butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and saute until translucent and soft, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and green chilies (if using), and cook for another 1-2 minutes until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Gravy
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews, sugar, 1 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, and salt to taste. Bring the mixture to a simmer, then cover and cook for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have broken down and the sauce thickens slightly.

instructions.title: Blend the Gravy
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the cooked gravy mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth and creamy. For an extra silky texture, you may pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan. This step is crucial for authentic Murgh Makhani consistency.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Gravy
instructions.text: Return the smooth gravy to the pan over medium-low heat. Add the partially cooked chicken pieces to the gravy. Stir well to combine, ensuring the chicken is fully coated. Simmer for another 8-10 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and tender, and the flavors have melded.

instructions.title: Finish the Butter Chicken
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed fenugreek leaves (Kasuri Methi), the remaining 1 tbsp butter, and heavy cream. Mix gently until the butter melts and the cream is incorporated. Do not boil after adding cream. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Transfer the Butter Chicken to a serving bowl. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with naan bread, roti, or steamed basmati rice for a complete and delicious Indian meal.

tips: For truly tender chicken, marinate it overnight. The yogurt helps to tenderize the meat beautifully.
Do not skip blending and straining the gravy. This is key to achieving the signature smooth, restaurant-quality texture of Butter Chicken.
Always add cream and butter at the very end of cooking and avoid boiling afterwards to prevent the cream from splitting and maintain its rich flavor.
article:
Mastering the Art of Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani: Your Ultimate Indian Lunch Guide

Few dishes evoke the warmth, richness, and culinary comfort quite like Butter Chicken, known in Hindi as Murgh Makhani. This iconic Indian lunch recipe has transcended borders, captivating palates worldwide with its luscious, creamy tomato-based gravy and tender, succulent pieces of marinated chicken. Far more than just a meal, Butter Chicken is an experience – a symphony of mild spices, tang, and sweetness that makes it an absolute favorite for family dinners, celebratory feasts, and, of course, a truly satisfying lunch. If you’ve ever dreamed of recreating this restaurant-quality dish in your own kitchen, you’re in the right place. Get ready to embark on a delicious journey to master the art of Murgh Makhani.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?

At its heart, Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) is a quintessential North Indian dish, originating from Delhi in the 1950s. The story goes that Kundan Lal Gujral, the founder of Moti Mahal restaurant, created this dish by combining leftover tandoori chicken with a rich, buttery tomato gravy. The name "Murgh Makhani" literally translates to "chicken with butter," a fitting description for a dish where butter and cream play starring roles in creating its signature velvety texture and indulgent flavor.

Unlike many spicier Indian curries, Butter Chicken is celebrated for its mild, subtly sweet, and tangy profile. The chicken is typically marinated in yogurt and spices before being cooked, often in a tandoor (traditional clay oven) or pan-fried, ensuring it remains incredibly moist. This cooked chicken is then simmered in a vibrant, smooth gravy made from tomatoes, cashews, ginger, garlic, and a delicate balance of aromatic spices like garam masala, fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), and a generous amount of butter and cream. It’s this unique combination that makes Butter Chicken such a universally beloved Indian lunch recipe, offering comfort and exquisite taste in every spoonful.

## Ingredients You Need

To create an authentic and mouth-watering Butter Chicken, gather the following ingredients. Quality ingredients make all the difference!

**For the Chicken Marinade:**
*   1 kg boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1.5-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 tsp red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color)
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1/2 tsp garam masala
*   1 tbsp lemon juice
*   Salt to taste

**For the Gravy:**
*   4 tbsp unsalted butter
*   2 large onions, finely chopped
*   2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 kg ripe tomatoes, pureed or 2 cans (400g each) crushed tomatoes
*   1/2 cup raw cashews, soaked in warm water for 30 minutes (optional, but adds creaminess)
*   1/2 cup tomato paste (for deeper flavor and color)
*   1 tsp red chili powder
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp garam masala
*   1 tbsp kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   1/2 cup heavy cream
*   1 tbsp sugar or honey (to balance tanginess)
*   Salt to taste
*   1/2 cup water or chicken broth (as needed for consistency)

**For Garnishing:**
*   Fresh coriander leaves, chopped
*   A swirl of fresh cream

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps carefully to achieve a truly spectacular Butter Chicken.

**1. Marinate the Chicken:**
In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring every piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours for maximum flavor and tenderness.

**2. Cook the Chicken:**
Heat 2 tablespoons of butter or oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the marinated chicken in batches (do not overcrowd the pan). Cook until nicely browned and almost cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per batch. Set aside.

**3. Prepare the Gravy Base:**
In the same pan, add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter. Once melted, add the chopped onions and sauté until softened and translucent, about 8-10 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another 2 minutes until fragrant.

**4. Build the Gravy:**
Stir in the tomato puree/crushed tomatoes and tomato paste. Add the red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well and cook for 10-15 minutes over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes break down and the mixture thickens, and the oil starts to separate from the sides.

**5. Blend for Smoothness:**
If using cashews, drain them and add them to the tomato mixture. Remove the pan from heat and let it cool slightly. Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender or use an immersion blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra velvety texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot.

**6. Simmer and Finish:**
Return the smooth gravy to the pot over medium-low heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces. Stir in the kasoori methi, sugar/honey, and water/broth (if needed to adjust consistency). Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to finish cooking in the sauce.

**7. The Grand Finale:**
Finally, stir in the heavy cream. Do not boil vigorously after adding cream, just let it warm through. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect, restaurant-style Butter Chicken isn't just about following a recipe; it's about understanding the nuances.

*   **Marination is Key:** The longer you marinate the chicken, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight is ideal.
*   **Sear the Chicken:** Don’t skip browning the chicken. This creates a delicious crust and locks in moisture, adding depth to the final dish.
*   **Blend, Blend, Blend:** A truly smooth gravy is the hallmark of authentic Murgh Makhani. Don't rush the blending process, and straining it ensures no fibrous bits remain.
*   **Kashmiri Chili Powder:** Use Kashmiri chili powder for its vibrant red color and mild heat. It gives the Butter Chicken its characteristic hue without making it overly spicy.
*   **Kasoori Methi:** This dried fenugreek leaf is non-negotiable. Crush it between your palms before adding to release its aromatic, slightly bitter, and sweet notes that are essential to the dish's flavor profile.
*   **Balance Sweet and Tangy:** The sugar or honey helps balance the acidity of the tomatoes. Adjust to your preference.
*   **Good Quality Butter and Cream:** Since these are star ingredients, using high-quality butter and fresh heavy cream will significantly enhance the richness and texture of your Butter Chicken.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of accompaniments, making it a complete and satisfying Indian lunch.

*   **Rice:** The classic pairing is fluffy Basmati rice. The rice soaks up the rich gravy beautifully.
*   **Bread:** Warm naan bread (garlic naan is exceptional!), roti, or paratha are perfect for scooping up every last drop of the delicious sauce.
*   **Raita:** A cooling cucumber or mint raita (yogurt dip) provides a refreshing contrast to the richness of the curry.
*   **Salad and Pickle:** A simple kachumber salad (diced cucumber, tomato, onion) and a side of Indian pickle can add a fresh crunch and tangy bite.
*   **Garnish:** Always finish with a swirl of fresh cream and a sprinkle of chopped fresh coriander leaves for both visual appeal and a burst of freshness.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken is a masterpiece, there are many ways to adapt it to dietary needs or personal preferences.

*   **Vegetarian Murgh Makhani:** Easily transform this into Paneer Makhani by substituting chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese) cubes. You can also use firm tofu or mixed vegetables like cauliflower and peas.
*   **Dairy-Free:** For a dairy-free version, use olive oil or a plant-based butter alternative. Replace heavy cream with full-fat coconut milk or a cashew cream (made by blending soaked cashews with water).
*   **Spice Level:** If you prefer more heat, increase the amount of red chili powder or add a finely chopped green chili to the gravy.
*   **Smoky Flavor (Dhungar):** For an authentic touch, especially if you don't have a tandoor, try the 'dhungar' method. Heat a small piece of charcoal until red hot, place it in a small bowl in the center of the gravy, drizzle a teaspoon of ghee over it, and immediately cover the pot for 5-10 minutes. This infuses the curry with a delightful smoky aroma.
*   **Make Ahead:** The gravy can be made a day or two in advance and stored in the refrigerator. Reheat gently and add the cooked chicken and cream just before serving. This allows the flavors to deepen even further.

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is more than just an Indian lunch recipe; it's a culinary hug in a bowl. With this comprehensive guide, you now have all the tools and tips to confidently create this beloved dish in your own kitchen. Prepare to impress your family and friends with a rich, creamy, and irresistibly delicious meal that truly tastes like it came from your favorite Indian restaurant. Enjoy the process, and savor every spoonful of your homemade masterpiece!
